hypothesis: Introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic residues at position 11 will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions with ECL2/ECL3 and TM5/TM6, while avoiding off-target interactions. A negatively charged residue at position 6 will disrupt off-target electrostatic complementarity while being tolerated in the SSTR2 pocket.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis is that SSTR2 selectivity can be achieved by optimizing non-pharmacophore positions to reduce off-target interactions. The next iteration will focus on these positions with targeted mutations to push Δmargin further above zero.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are non-pharmacophore and are known to contribute to off-target binding in SSTR3/SSTR5. Focusing mutations here can disrupt conserved pocket interactions while preserving key pharmacophore residues.
These mutations are designed to break hydrogen bonds and hydrophobic interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 while maintaining flexibility and compatibility with SSTR2.
